evidence_quotes.quote: strategies used by undertakings using dark pedestrians.

paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. In my capacity as Jean Monnet and EU Citizen, I believe that the Digital Fairness Act can bring a big change in the digital market towards consumers, and above all the new generations, as we have grown in a completely technological world, experiencing first-hand the challenges and risks this entails. In the digital environment, the protection of minors is a key aspect as they consume large online content without sufficient knowledge of the risks this entails in relation to advertising or information manipulation. I therefore consider that this law can become very helpful in ensuring transparency and preventing practices that could be detrimental to young people. On the other hand, on the basis of the definition of average consumer, who must be reasonably informed, reasonably observant and circumspect, I have made me aware that it is often left unprotected against strategies used by undertakings using dark pedestrians. This is why I believe this law is crucial to ensure that everyone can interact much more safely with the digital market, increasing their trust in businesses. To conclude, it is very important for me that a world where the use of technological means is essential for everyday life, it is imperative that consumers understand the services they are using and how businesses process data. This law would not only protect consumers from abuses that could be committed by entrepreneurs, but also foster a culture of more transparency and trust among businesses and users, promoting more responsible business practices.
